# 1. Primary: Systolic Blood Pressure observations > 140 mmHg (final/amended results, with Patient data)
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8480-6&value-quantity=gt140||mm[Hg]&status=final,amended&_include=Observation:subject&_include=Observation:encounter

# 2. Recent SBP > 140 mmHg (last 12 months — current "uncontrolled" window)
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8480-6&value-quantity=gt140||mm[Hg]&status=final,amended&date=ge2025-06-02&_include=Observation:subject

# 3. Active Hypertension diagnoses (SNOMED CT) — confirms "diagnosed" hypertension
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|38341003&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject

# 4. Active Hypertension diagnoses (ICD-10-CM) — Essential hypertension
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I10&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject

# 5. Active antihypertensive MedicationRequests — confirms "despite treatment"
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1091643,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|29046,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|214354,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|18867&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject

# 6. Patients with SBP > 140 AND active hypertension condition (reverse include)
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|38341003,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I10&clinical-status=active&_has:Observation:subject:code=http://loinc.org|8480-6&_has:Observation:subject:value-quantity=gt140||mm[Hg]
```

---

### Key Parameters Explained

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| **LOINC `8480-6`** | Systolic Blood Pressure (component measurement) |
| **`value-quantity=gt140\|\|mm[Hg]`** | Greater than 140, UCUM unit `mm[Hg]` |
| **`status=final,amended`** | Excludes preliminary/entered-in-error results |
| **SNOMED `38341003`** | Hypertensive disorder, systemic arterial |
| **ICD-10-CM `I10`** | Essential (primary) hypertension |
| **RxNorm codes** | Amlodipine (29046), Lisinopril (29046), Losartan (203160), Metoprolol (41493) — extend as needed |
| **`_has`** | Reverse chaining to find Patients/Conditions linked to qualifying Observations |

> 💡 **Tip:** Combine queries 3/4 + 5 with a patient `_id` list derived from query 1/2 to precisely identify patients with **confirmed diagnosis + active medication + persistent elevation**.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients with systolic blood pressure (SBP) measurements above 140 mmHg, indicating uncontrolled hypertension despite treatment.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8480-6&value-quantity=gt140||mm[Hg]&category=vital-signs
```

**Query breakdown:**

| Component | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `ResourceType` | `Observation` | Vital-sign measurements live here in FHIR R4 |
| `code` | `http://loinc.org\|8480-6` | LOINC **8480-6** = *Systolic blood pressure* — the canonical vital-signs code used by PheKB HTN phenotypes |
| `value-quantity` | `gt140\|\|mm[Hg]` | `gt` (strictly greater than) 140 in UCUM millimetres of mercury; omitting the system URI segment (`||`) keeps the comparator unit-system-agnostic while still filtering by UCUM unit |
| `category` | `vital-signs` | Scopes to the standard vital-signs category (`http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category\|vital-signs`), reducing noise from non-vital-sign BP observations |

**Important caveats for the full PheKB Type-2 Resistant HTN phenotype:**

1. **Multi-drug antihypertensives required** — pair this query with `MedicationRequest` (or `MedicationStatement`) searches for ≥3 concurrent antihypertensive drug classes (e.g., ACE inhibitors, ARBs, thiazides, CCBs, beta-blockers) to satisfy the "resistant" criterion.
2. **Temporality** — PheKB requires the elevated BP and the multi-drug therapy to overlap in time; use `date` parameters or post-query logic to enforce concurrent windows.
3. **Component observations** — some servers store BP as a single `Observation` with `component` codes (systolic = 8480-6, diastolic = 8462-4). If this query returns no results, also try omitting `category` or querying the parent BP panel code (`55284-4`).

| Type | Criteria | FHIR Sources |

## FHIR Queries: Four Separate Drug-Class Queries for Resistant HTN (PheKB Type-1)

Each query targets one antihypertensive class at the RxNorm **ingredient** level, capturing all formulations and strengths. Patient cohort intersection (`subject` references common to all four result sets) is performed post-query.

---

### Query 1 — ACE Inhibitor (Lisinopril, RxNorm `29046`)
```
MedicationRequest?medication=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|29046&status=active&intent=order&_elements=subject,medication,authoredOn
```

### Query 2 — Calcium Channel Blocker (Amlodipine, RxNorm `17767`)
```
MedicationRequest?medication=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|17767&status=active&intent=order&_elements=subject,medication,authoredOn
```

### Query 3 — Thiazide Diuretic (Hydrochlorothiazide, RxNorm `5487`)
```
MedicationRequest?medication=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5487&status=active&intent=order&_elements=subject,medication,authoredOn
```

### Query 4 — Aldosterone Antagonist (Spironolactone, RxNorm `9997`)
```
MedicationRequest?medication=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9997&status=active&intent=order&_elements=subject,medication,authoredOn
```

---

## Design Rationale

| Parameter | Value | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `medication=` | `http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm\|<code>` | Ingredient-level RxNorm code captures all brand names, strengths, and dose forms for that class |
| `status=active` | — | Restricts to current, concurrent prescriptions (required for "concurrent use" criterion) |
| `intent=order` | — | Excludes proposals/plans; targets actionable prescriptions |
| `_elements=subject,medication,authoredOn` | — | Returns only the fields needed for patient-ID intersection and temporality checks |

## Post-Query Intersection Logic

```
Resistant_HTN_Type1_Cohort =
  patients(Query1) ∩ patients(Query2) ∩ patients(Query3) ∩ patients(Query4)
```

> ⚠️ **Combination-pill edge case:** A patient on lisinopril/HCTZ (fixed-dose combination, e.g., RxNorm `314076`) would satisfy two drug classes via one prescription. Consider adding an `OR` search across combination product RxNorm codes within Queries 1 and 3 if your formulary includes fixed-dose combos.

> ⚠️ **`authoredOn` overlap check:** For strict PheKB concurrency, filter intersected results so that all four `authoredOn` (or `dispenseRequest.validityPeriod`) windows overlap — preventing sequential (non-concurrent) regimens from inflating the cohort.
